posts.title: LeetCode-84 Largest Rectangle in Histogram
posts.text: Description Given n non-negative integers representing the histogram’s bar height where the width of each bar is 1, find the area of largest rectangle in the histogram. SolutionThis problem is definitely hard, but there is still a elegant solution. For every index i of array arr, we maintain two pointer, let’s say it right and left, left points to an index in the left of i where arr[left] <= arr[i], and right points to an index in the right where arr[right] <= arr[i]. For every index i, we find these two index, so we create two array, lessFromLeft and lessFromRight, to store these two index. So, the problem is how to calculate these two arrays. The straight-forward method is use nested loop , which leads to O(n^2) time complexity. For example :123456789var lessFromLeft []intfor i := 1; i < length; i++ { p := i -1 for p >=0 && array[p] >= array[i] { p-- } lessFromLeft[i] = p} We can optomize above code, changing time complexity from O(n^2 to O(n). This is how we do it: for index i, we have already calculated lessFromLeft[i-1], so if array[i] <= array[i-1, we just skip to lessFromLeft[i-1] to reduce calculation, code as follows:123456789var lessFromLeft []intfor i := 1; i < length; i++ { p := i -1 for p >=0 && array[p] >= array[i] { p = lessFromLeft[p] } lessFromLeft[i] = p}So our final solution is here, with time complexity O(n) and space complexity O(n).1234567891011121314151617181920212223242526272829303132333435363738func largestRectangleArea(heights []int) int { var maxArea int if len(heights) == 0 { return maxArea } length := len(heights) lessFromLeft := make([]int,length) lessFromRight := make([]int,length) lessFromLeft[0],lessFromRight[length-1]=-1,length // used in loop for termination // calculate lessFromLeft for i := 1; i < length;i++ { p := i-1 for p >= 0 && heights[p] >= heights[i] { p = lessFromLeft[p] } lessFromLeft[i] = p } // calculate lessFromRight for i := length-2; i>=0;i-- { p := i + 1 for p < length && heights[p] >= heights[i] { p = lessFromRight[p] } lessFromRight[i] = p } // calculate max area for i := 0; i< length;i++ { maxArea = max(maxArea, heights[i] * (lessFromRight[i]-lessFromLeft[i]-1)) } return maxArea}

posts.title: LeetCode-88 Merge Sorted Array
posts.text: Description Given two sorted integer arrays nums1 and nums2, merge nums2 into nums1 as one sorted array. Note: The number of elements initialized in nums1 and nums2 are m and n respectively. You may assume that nums1 has enough space (size that is equal to m + n) to hold additional elements from nums2. Example: 12345Input:nums1 = [1,2,3,0,0,0], m = 3nums2 = [2,5,6], n = 3Output: [1,2,2,3,5,6] SolutionThis is a typical merge sort. The trick is we need to append from back to front. Time complexity is O(n). Code as follows:1234567891011121314151617181920class Solution {public: void merge(vector<int>& nums1, int m, vector<int>& nums2, int n) { int i = m-1, j =n-1; int total = m+n -1; while(i >= 0 && j >= 0) { if (nums1[i] > nums2[j]) { nums1[total--] = nums1[i--]; } else { nums1[total--] = nums2[j--]; } } // we do not need to care about nums1, cause the remaining values are sorted in nums1 while (j >= 0) { nums1[total--] = nums2[j--]; } }};
